basics.name: Chris J Mears
basics.label: Senior Software & Analytics Engineer
basics.summary: Senior Software and Analytics Engineer with 20 years of experience building production systems from the ground up and transforming complex datasets into actionable insights. Expert in architecting full-stack applications with Svelte and TypeScript, and designing high-integrity data pipelines using Python, SQL, and BigQuery. Currently a Graduate Teaching Assistant at Georgia Tech mentoring 1,500+ students in data analysis, with a proven track record of owning technical strategy, infrastructure decisions, and end-to-end product delivery in healthcare and startup environments.

work.name: Georgia Institute of Technology (Remote)
work.position: Graduate Teaching Assistant (CSE 6040: Computing for Data Analysis)
work.summary: Mentoring 1,500+ graduate students in Python, Pandas, SQL, and NumPy, adapting explanations to individual learning levels and building tutorial videos to reduce repetitive support needs.
highlights: Mentor 1500+ graduate students in Python, Pandas, SQL, and NumPy, adapting explanations to individual learning levels and building tutorial videos to reduce repetitive support needs.

work.name: Corvia Medical (Remote)
work.summary: Ensuring data integrity for medical device research by manually digitizing and validating analog pressure waveform signals into ground-truth datasets for downstream analysis.
highlights: Ensuring data integrity for medical device research by manually digitizing and validating analog pressure waveform signals into ground-truth datasets for downstream analysis.

work.name: Definitive Healthcare / Carevoyance
work.summary: Implemented and scheduled Node.js data pipelines to transform billions of Medicare and proprietary claims into BigQuery and import curated datasets into PostgreSQL for analytics and in‑product use.
highlights: Achieved 90%+ auto-match rate on post-acquisition entity resolution across two healthcare databases — reverse-engineered undocumented address waterfall logic from the pipeline (interviewing stakeholders to reconstruct decision history), then designed NPI-based BigQuery matching queries that cut cloud testing costs ~90% per run while processing billions of Medicare claims records.

Implemented data pipelines to transform billions of Medicare and proprietary claims data, load into BigQuery, and import into PostgreSQL via scheduled Node.js scripts, enhancing data accessibility for analytics and in-product usage.

work.name: H1 / Carevoyance
work.summary: Built Svelte reporting UIs and Express.js APIs that delivered executive‑level healthcare analytics; extended a Python/TypeScript PowerPoint generation system with data‑driven charts; managed Elasticsearch and a custom graph database while overseeing AWS and GCP services.
highlights: Saved the analysis team 10+ hours/week (25%+ productivity increase) by architecting a full-stack reporting engine (Python/TypeScript) that auto-generated branded, white-labeled PowerPoint deliverables from Elasticsearch and PostgreSQL — working around Office XML constraints to build an iteration pipeline, eliminating the need for customers to export data to external tools.

Delivered client-facing Looker dashboards used for 1-2 years — built the underlying data infrastructure (BigQuery, GCS staging, CTEs) that continued powering analytics after dashboards were absorbed into the platform.
Increased daily platform utilization by serving as primary frontend implementer — translated complex UI/UX designs into production-ready Svelte and TypeScript applications across the entire platform.
Resolved client issues end-to-end without escalation by maintaining and querying Elasticsearch clusters, MongoDB, and PostgreSQL directly supporting customer service.

work.name: Carevoyance
work.summary: Early engineering hire at a twice-acquired healthcare claims analytics startup, contributing to the development of innovative solutions through data visualization, UI architecture, and data pipeline integration.
highlights: Reduced manual database maintenance by 50% by writing Node.js and Bash automation scripts for Elasticsearch cluster operations — scripting patterns later used as teaching examples for automation and data engineering.
Generated comprehensive reports using Svelte and an Express.js API server on key healthcare business metrics, including Total ROI, Readmissions, and procedure referrals analysis.
Engineered initial UI architecture and data visualization projects using AngularJS and Svelte, resulting in a 20% increase in user engagement.
Created Google Looker Studio dashboards from daily-updated GCS data, transforming complex user analytics datasets from PostgreSQL into actionable visualizations.

work.name: Wandering Leaf Studios, LLC
work.position: Owner / Software Engineer / Data Engineer / Data Analyst
work.summary: Established a consulting business that empowers clients to discover hidden data insights, increasing actionable strategies by 30% through tailored analytics and technology solutions.
highlights: Generated $200k in incremental online sales for an e-commerce client by building custom Shopify bundle integrations and JavaScript-based product configurators that increased average order value.
Ran geo-targeted A/B tests on Google Ads to optimize ad creative and audience segmentation — establishing foundational experimentation practices for data-driven marketing decisions.
Cut manual operations by 50% by developing automation pipelines using Zapier and cloud-based scraping tools — real-world automation workflow design applicable to data engineering curricula.

work.name: Amazon / Goodreads
work.summary: Hired pre-Amazon acquisition and contributor to mobile web development team on a product used by millions of users.
highlights: Served 17M+ monthly unique visitors by building high-traffic, cross-browser/device mobile web applications using Ruby on Rails and JavaScript.
Mentored junior developers on frontend best practices through structured peer reviews and code quality sessions.

work.name: Canned Banners
work.summary: Technical co-founder at a three-person bootstrapped advertising tech startup that culminated in a successful acquisition by Adacus in January 2018.
highlights: Built a scalable SaaS advertising platform from scratch (PHP, Ruby on Rails, AWS) — owned all technical strategy, infrastructure decisions, and end-to-end product delivery.

work.name: Cisco Systems
work.summary: Initially hired as a contract-to-hire Software Engineer III, transitioning to a full-time Front-End Engineer role, contributing to optimizing user experience and enhancing the performance of enterprise-level media solutions.
highlights: Built and maintained frontend media pages for a major entertainment platform using PHP and CSS — led internationalization efforts for global deployment, gaining early exposure to i18n workflows and cross-market content delivery.

work.name: Foodbuzz, Inc
work.summary: Transitioned to a Full-stack Web Developer role, focusing on establishing robust front-end solutions and fostering best practices in coding standards within a startup environment.
highlights: Established the front-end web development framework for a small startup, initiating team coding standards, code quality, code review, and collaboration.

work.name: Wine Institute
work.summary: Pioneered the IT department as the first hire at the Wine Institute, driving significant improvements in technology infrastructure and operations within a non-profit environment.
highlights: Served as first IT manager — migrated member database to a CRM-based system for improved tracking, redesigned the public-facing website, and managed external contractors for web development projects.

work.name: Progressive Insurance
work.summary: Acquired technical expertise in telecommunications and network support, focusing on data management, security protocols, and customer service in a corporate environment.
highlights: Provided tier-1 network support and troubleshooting for enterprise off-site locations — escalated to telecom carriers and software-level diagnostics, gaining foundational experience in operational monitoring and incident response.

work.name: NCR
work.summary: Engaged in a focused internship at NCR, contributing to the implementation of network solutions and enhancing customer connectivity.
highlights: Assisted in deploying a VPN program to over 1,000 employees and customers, enhancing secure access and connectivity for clients within NPR.

work.name: BAE Systems
work.summary: Gained hands-on experience in web development, collaborating with a corporate team to deliver critical applications for defense clients.
highlights: Collaborated with a corporate web team to develop 3 ColdFusion web applications for the U.S. Navy (AEGIS) and Defense Energy Support Center (DESC), enhancing operational efficiency and user experience.

courses: Computing for Data Analysis (Python, Pandas, SQLite, NumPy, scikit-learn)
Business Fundamentals for Analytics: Accounting, Supply Chain, Financing, Marketing, Strategy
Minor in Business Administration; Calculus, Data Networks

certificates.name: Data Science Career Track, Data Science (April 2020 cohort)
certificates.summary: 600+ hours of hands-on curriculum, with 1:1 industry expert mentor oversight, and completion of 2 in-depth capstone projects. Mastering skills in Python, SQL, data analysis, data visualization, hypothesis testing, and machine learning.

skills.name: Data & AI
keywords: Statistics (hypothesis testing, t-tests, confidence intervals, p-values, regression analysis, probability, distributions)

projects.name: LLM Evaluation Pipeline
projects.description: Systematically comparing distilled and quantized local models (Llama CPP, LM Studio) against API baselines — discovering that quantization introduces behavioral instability (repetition loops, hallucinations) beyond mere accuracy degradation, with implications for deploying LLMs in resource-constrained clinical NLP and medical document processing environments.

projects.name: Chicago Housing Predictive Visualization
projects.description: Full-stack analytics tool (D3.js, Leaflet, Svelte) forecasting housing flip probabilities from the Chicago public datasets.

projects.name: Open Brewery DB
projects.description: Creator and maintainer of an open-source dataset and API serving 800k+ weekly requests across 10+ years — the go-to resource for global brewery data with 70+ community contributors. Migrated from Rails/PostgreSQL to Laravel/SQLite with Meilisearch for real-time search, reducing infrastructure cost while scaling. Survived 10x traffic spikes through caching-first architecture. Currently building data quality pipelines to track brewery lifecycle events (openings, closures, relocations) across historical records.

projects.name: Brewery Review Analysis with Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Named Entity Recognition (NER)
projects.description: Springboard capstone — transformer-based NLP and NER pipeline for sentiment classification and entity extraction from brewery reviews; NER methodology directly transferable to clinical entity extraction.

projects.name: Credit Card Fraud Analysis and Prediction
projects.description: Built a machine learning pipeline (scikit-learn) to detect fraudulent transactions with severe class imbalance — optimized for recall over precision based on cost asymmetry (missing fraud is more expensive than investigating false positives), using SMOTE to improve minority class detection.
